options.name: --version
options.description: Display the version string and exit.

options.name: --output
options.description:
Output solution file. Defaults to a solution file at
the root of the supplied base directory.

options.name: --include-projects
options.description:
Included project files globbing pattern. Multiple
arguments are treated using OR semantics.

options.name: --exclude-projects
options.description: Excluded project files globbing pattern. Multiple

options.name: --include-files
options.description: Included solution files globbing pattern. Multiple

options.name: --exclude-files
options.description: Excluded solution files globbing pattern. Multiple

options.name: --no-git-ignore
options.description:
Do not take .gitignore files into account when
excluding files.

options.name: --no-files
options.description:
Do not include any solution items in the generated
solution.

options.name: --no-transitive-projects
options.description:
By default, nosln will include transitive p2p
dependencies, even if excluded by a globbing pattern
or outside of the project directory.Enable this flag
to avoid expanding to transitive projects.

options.name: --absolute-paths
options.description:
Use absolute paths in generated solution file.
Otherwise contents will be relative to the solution
file output folder.

options.name: --flatten
options.description:
Places all projects at the root of the solution file
without replicating the filesystem structure. Also
implies --no-files.

options.name: --start
options.description:
Starts an IDE process with the newly generated
solution file. Uses 'start' command on Windows,
'open' on OSX, 'xdg-open' on Linux.

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
Quiet mode, only output the file name of the
generated solution to stdout. Useful for passing
generated solution to shell scripts.

options.name: --temp
options.description:
Creates a disposable solution file in the system temp
folder. Overrides the --output argument. Also implies
